summary: The review of the "Service Provider Agreement Example.pdf" indicates a moderate risk posture overall, with several clauses flagged for further review or escalation. Key issues include overly broad indemnity obligations (red flag) and deviations from UoA's Preferred Positions on confidentiality, intellectual property, and termination rights (amber flags). Immediate next steps should involve escalating flagged clauses to the appropriate UoA teams, such as the Senior Manager for Research Grants and Contracts and the Head of IP at UniServices, to ensure compliance with institutional policies and mitigate potential risks.

flags.clause_title: (c) all other liabilities and expenses of whatever nature relating to the Service
flags.snippet: (c) all other liabilities and expenses of whatever nature relating to the Service Provider. The Service Provider indemnifies the Company against all such taxes, levies, insurance premiums and other expenses.
flags.rationale: The clause imposes an indemnity obligation on the Service Provider for 'all other liabilities and expenses of whatever nature,' which is overly broad and undefined. This conflicts with UoA's Preferred Contracting Position on indemnities (POS-08), which explicitly limits indemnities to damage/injury caused by negligent acts/omissions or wilful misconduct, and requires liability under indemnities to be capped within the total liability limit. Additionally, indemnities against taxes, levies, and insurance premiums are not addressed in the UoA Positions and are outside the scope of acceptable indemnity terms. Escalation to Auckland UniServices Ltd or the Research Grants and Contracts Delegations is required.
flags.text:
(c) all other liabilities and expenses of whatever nature relating to the Service
Provider.
The Service Provider indemnifies the Company against all such taxes, levies, insurance
premiums and other expenses.

flags.clause_title: (f) not do anything to prejudice the good name of the Company in any dealings with
flags.snippet: (f) not do anything to prejudice the good name of the Company in any dealings with third parties including clients/customers;
flags.rationale: This clause falls under the 'No Disrepute Clauses' topic (POS-20). UoA's Preferred Position explicitly rejects clauses that preclude academic staff or students from actions that might bring the client into 'disrepute'. The clause does not include the required statutory academic freedom wording ('Nothing in this Agreement will limit the rights of the University's personnel to exercise the academic freedoms enshrined in s267 of the Education and Training Act 2020 (NZ)'). Without this safeguard, the clause materially deviates from UoA's Preferred Position and triggers escalation to the Senior Manager Research Grants and Contracts.
flags.text:
(f) not do anything to prejudice the good name of the Company in any dealings with
third parties including clients/customers;

flags.clause_title: 2.1 The Service Provider’s appointment will commence on the date specified in Item 6
flags.snippet: 2.1 The Service Provider’s appointment will commence on the date specified in Item 6 of the Schedule and, subject to clauses 2.6 and 9, will terminate on the date specified in Item 7 of the Schedule without the need for either party to give the other party any notice.
flags.rationale: The clause touches on termination but does not explicitly align with UoA's Preferred Position for mutual two-way termination for material breach or insolvency, nor does it provide a clear right for UoA to terminate for failure to pay. The absence of notice for termination raises concerns about accrued rights and benefits, as well as termination costs, which are critical under UoA Position #POS-19. While the clause does not outright contradict the Acceptable tier, it requires further review to ensure compliance with UoA's termination standards.
flags.text:
2.1 The Service Provider’s appointment will commence on the date specified in Item 6 of
the Schedule and, subject to clauses 2.6 and 9, will terminate on the date specified in
Item 7 of the Schedule without the need for either party to give the other party any
notice.

flags.clause_title: 2.3 The parties agree that the Service Provider’s Key Personnel defined in Item 10 of
flags.snippet: 2.3 The parties agree that the Service Provider’s Key Personnel defined in Item 10 of the Schedule, are the person(s) to provide the Services on behalf of the Service Provider. Any variation to the Service Provider’s Key Personnel must be approved in writing by the Company. Either party may terminate this Agreement on notice to the other party if the parties fail to agree a replacement Key Personnel within 30 days of the Company’s receipt of the Service Provider’s notice under this clause 2.3.
flags.rationale: This clause touches on termination rights, which are sensitive under UoA Position #POS-19. While the clause allows termination for failure to agree on replacement Key Personnel, it does not explicitly align with UoA's preferred position of mutual two-way termination for material breach or insolvency, nor does it address accrued rights or termination costs. However, it does not fall outside the acceptable tier, as termination for convenience is not explicitly invoked, and the clause is specific to personnel replacement. Escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts is recommended for further review.
flags.text:
2.3 The parties agree that the Service Provider’s Key Personnel defined in Item 10 of the
Schedule, are the person(s) to provide the Services on behalf of the Service Provider.
Any variation to the Service Provider’s Key Personnel must be approved in writing by
the Company. Either party may terminate this Agreement on notice to the other party
if the parties fail to agree a replacement Key Personnel within 30 days of the Company’s
receipt of the Service Provider’s notice under this clause 2.3.

flags.clause_title: (b) developed independently of this Agreement and any information of the
flags.snippet: (b) developed independently of this Agreement and any information of the Disclosing Party; or received by the Recipient from a third party without restriction and without any breach of any obligation of confidentiality.
flags.rationale: The clause touches on the topic of Confidentiality, specifically the definition of confidential information. While it aligns partially with UoA Position #POS-11 by excluding information independently developed or received from a third party without restriction, it does not explicitly address UoA's preferred position regarding public information, previously known information, or legally required disclosures. Additionally, the clause does not specify the duration of confidentiality obligations, which is a critical aspect of UoA's preferred and acceptable tiers. This deviation places the clause in the amber tier, requiring confirmation or adjustment by the Contract Manager.
flags.text:
(b) developed independently of this Agreement and any information of the
Disclosing Party; or received by the Recipient from a third party without
restriction and without any breach of any obligation of confidentiality.
Data Outputs means the output of the Algorithm in the form of lists of raw product
recommendations (inventory choices) for each iteration of product orders.
Documentation means details of the algorithm processes (but not the code) and
processing environment.

flags.clause_title: (a) its own liability for all taxes, payments, and levies under any relevant legislation
flags.snippet: (a) its own liability for all taxes, payments, and levies under any relevant legislation including the Income Tax Act 2007 and all accident compensation legislation;
flags.rationale: The clause addresses taxation, which is a UoA-sensitive topic under Position #POS-06. While it specifies liability for taxes and levies under relevant legislation, it does not explicitly align with the Preferred Position for NZ contracts ('costs presented as excluding GST') or overseas contracts ('costs excluding any applicable taxes and duties, payable by the Client on any taxable supplies'). The clause does not contradict the Position but lacks the specificity required for full alignment. Escalation to the Tax and Compliance team is recommended to confirm compliance with UoA's tax-related requirements.
flags.text:
(a) its own liability for all taxes, payments, and levies under any relevant legislation
including the Income Tax Act 2007 and all accident compensation legislation;

flags.clause_title: (b) registering for GST and complying with the Goods and Services Tax Act 1985 and
flags.snippet: (b) registering for GST and complying with the Goods and Services Tax Act 1985 and all GST requirements;
flags.rationale: The clause addresses GST compliance, which is relevant to UoA Position #POS-06 on Taxation. However, it does not explicitly align with the Preferred Position, which specifies that NZ contracts should present costs as 'excluding GST' and overseas contracts should present costs as 'excluding any applicable taxes and duties, payable by the Client on any taxable supplies.' The clause does not clarify whether these requirements are met, and further review by the Tax and Compliance team may be necessary to ensure compliance with UoA's tax-related standards.
flags.text:
(b) registering for GST and complying with the Goods and Services Tax Act 1985 and
all GST requirements;

flags.clause_title: Confidential Information of a party (Disclosing Party) means all information that is
flags.snippet: Confidential Information of a party (Disclosing Party) means all information that is about the Disclosing Party, its Affiliates, or its or its Affiliates’ affairs, or obtained by the other party (Recipient) directly or indirectly from the Disclosing Party or its Affiliates in connection with this Agreement, and includes the terms and conditions of this Agreement, but excludes information:
flags.rationale: The clause addresses the definition of Confidential Information but does not explicitly exclude information already public, independently developed, previously known by the receiving party, or legally required to be disclosed, as required by UoA's Preferred Position. Additionally, the clause does not specify the duration of confidentiality obligations (up to 5 years post-completion as per Preferred Position). These omissions place the clause in the Acceptable tier, requiring confirmation of compliance with UoA Position #POS-11.

Affiliates in connection with this Agreement, and includes the terms and conditions of

flags.clause_title: Improvement means any improvement, development, enhancement, modification or
flags.snippet: Improvement means any improvement, development, enhancement, modification or derivative of any product, design, process or Intellectual Property Right that cannot be used independently of such product, design, process o…
flags.rationale: The clause pertains to Intellectual Property, which is explicitly outside the scope of UoA Contracting Positions and must be referred to Auckland UniServices Ltd for review. UoA Position #POS-IP mandates that all IP clauses, regardless of wording, require referral to the Head of IP at UniServices.

flags.clause_title: Intellectual Property Rights means all intellectual property rights conferred by law
flags.snippet: Intellectual Property Rights means all intellectual property rights conferred by law and includes the rights to, and any interests in, any patent, trade mark, service mark, copyright, moral right, trade name, brand name…
flags.rationale: The clause pertains to Intellectual Property, which is explicitly excluded from UoA Contracting Positions and must be referred to Auckland UniServices Ltd (Head of IP) for review. This aligns with UoA Position #POS-IP, which mandates referral of all IP clauses regardless of wording.
flags.text:
Intellectual Property Rights means all intellectual property rights conferred by law
and includes the rights to, and any interests in, any patent, trade mark, service mark,
copyright, moral right, trade name, brand name, logo, corporate dress, right to use,
domain name, social media presence, handle and account, technical information, right
in computer software, databases and lists, right in inventions, right in a design, know-
how, trade secret, confidential information and any other proprietary right or form of
intellectual property rights (whether or not registered or capable of registration), and
the right to register, renew or extend any of the foregoing rights;

flags.clause_title: This Agreement is made on the date set out in Item 1 of the Schedule
flags.snippet: This Agreement is made on the date set out in Item 1 of the Schedule Between (1) The company whose name appears in Item 2 of the Schedule (the Company) And The University of Auckland (2) The company whose name appears i…

flags.text:
This Agreement is made on the date set out in Item 1 of the Schedule
Between
(1) The company whose name appears in Item 2 of the Schedule (the Company)
And The University of Auckland
(2) The company whose name appears in Item 3 of the Schedule (the
Service Provider).
Introduction
A. The Company wishes to appoint the Service Provider to carry out certain services and
the Service Provider has agreed to accept such appointment.
B. The parties wish to record the terms and conditions upon which the Services will be
carried out.
C. The Service Provider has appointed its wholly-owned subsidiary, Auckland
UniServices Limited (UniServices), as its agent to procure and enter into agreements
on the Service Provider’s behalf in relation to the performance of these types of
services.
It is agreed

flags.clause_title: Working Day means a day on which businesses and banks in Auckland are generally
flags.snippet: Working Day means a day on which businesses and banks in Auckland are generally open for business.
flags.rationale: The clause defines 'Working Day,' which is not addressed in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or tied to any specific policy. As such, it falls outside the scope of the Positions document and does not conflict with any established UoA standards.

flags.clause_title: 3.3 The Service Provider agrees that this Agreement does not in any way prevent, restrict
flags.snippet: 3.3 The Service Provider agrees that this Agreement does not in any way prevent, restrict or limit the Company from engaging other persons to provide the same or similar services and that no claim shall like against the Company should the Company exercise such rights.
flags.rationale: This clause does not impose any liability on the University or materially deviate from UoA's Preferred or Acceptable positions on liability limitations and exclusions. It simply clarifies that the Company may engage other service providers without liability. There is no indication of liability exposure, indemnities, or indirect/consequential losses that would trigger escalation under UoA Position #POS-07.
flags.text:
3.3 The Service Provider agrees that this Agreement does not in any way prevent, restrict
or limit the Company from engaging other persons to provide the same or similar
services and that no claim shall like against the Company should the Company exercise
such rights.
4. Obligations of the Company
4.1 The Company will pay the Service Provider for the Services the fees and expenses set
out in Item 5 of the Schedule at the times and in the manner set out in clause 6 and
Item 5 of the Schedule on receipt of an itemised GST invoice from the Service Provider.
The Company shall make all necessary withholding tax deductions from the charges
payable to the Service Provider unless this is not required, or the Service Provider
supplies the Company with a Certificate of Exemption.
4.2 The Company will co-operate with the Service Provider and will provide the Service
Provider with all information in its possession which may be relevant to the provision
of the Services.
5. Progress meetings
5.1 During the term of this Agreement, the parties will hold progress meetings for the
purpose of allowing the Company and the Service Provider to discuss the performance
of the Services. These meetings will be held at such times and locations as the Company
may reasonably require.
6. Payment
6.1 If any invoice submitted by the Service Provider is disputed by the Company, the
Company will notify the Service Provider of the reasons for the dispute but will
otherwise pay that part of the invoice which is not disputed. Any disputed amount will
be resolved in accordance with clause 10.

6.2 The Service Provider will be responsible for all expenses incurred in the carrying out of
the Services unless as otherwise listed in the Schedule.
7. Intellectual Property & Confidential information
7.1 The Service Provider acknowledges the strict confidentiality and proprietary nature of
all Confidential Information and that no right, entitlement or interest in the Confidential
Information is extended to or conveyed to the Service Provider other than for the strict
purpose of performing the Services. The Service Provider also acknowledges that the
Service Provider may become aware of or be given Confidential Information which the
Company has invested considerable time and cost in obtaining and developing for the
purposes of its business. The Service Provider agrees that any breach of its obligations
of confidentiality in this Agreement would cause the Company to suffer serious and
irreparable loss and damage for which damages would not be an adequate remedy,
and in the event of any threatened, apparent or actual breach of this Agreement, the
Company is entitled to seek immediate injunctive relief.
7.2 The Service Provider undertakes that it will:
(a) only use the Confidential Information for the purpose of performing the Services,
and will not use, or attempt to use, in any way the Confidential Information for its
own purposes or the purposes of any third party;
(b) keep all of the Confidential Information confidential;
(c) except as provided under clause 7.2(e), not, without the prior written consent of
the Company, disclose in any form, the whole or any part of the Confidential
Information to any third person;
(d) at the request of the Company, and in any event on the termination of this
Agreement, promptly return to the Company all documents and other material
relating to the Confidential Information and all copies of those items;
(e) only disclose the Confidential Information to its employees (if any) directly
involved with the Service Provider in the fulfilment of the Service Provider’s
obligations under this Agreement, and/or to legal or accounting advisers for the
purpose of providing to the Service Provider legal or accounting advice in relation
to this Agreement, and in each case the Service Provider will ensure that such
employees and advisers are aware of, and comply with, these obligations as to
confidentiality; and

7.3 The obligations of confidentiality set out in clauses 7.1 and 7.2 will not apply to any
information which:
(a) the Service Provider can clearly show was independently available to the Service
Provider from a third party having the right to disclose it;
(b) at the time of execution of this Agreement is in the public domain, or
subsequently enters the public domain, through no fault of the Service Provider
or any other person the Service Provider has disclosed the information to or who
has obtained access to the information (whether directly or indirectly) through
the acts or omissions of the Service Provider;
(c) the Service Provider can show by written record is in the Service Provider’s lawful
possession and was not obtained by the Service Provider from the Company;
(d) is independently developed by the Service Provider without reference to the
Company’s Confidential Information; or
(e) the parties agree in writing will be exempted from the confidentiality provisions
of this Agreement.
7.4 If the Service Provider is required by law or an order of a court of competent jurisdiction
or any competent judicial, governmental or regulatory body to disclose any Confidential
Information, the Service Provider must:
(a) first inform the Company of this obligation;
(b) allow the Company reasonable time to avoid the disclosure having to be made;
(c) give the Company such assistance (at the Company’s cost) as the Company
reasonably requests in doing this; and
(d) only disclose such Confidential Information to the minimum extent required.
7.5 Each party acknowledges the strict confidentiality and proprietary nature of the other’s
Pre-existing IP and that no right, entitlement or interest in a party’s Pre-existing IP is
extended to or conveyed to the other party, except as expressly stated in this
Agreement.
7.6 Each party acknowledges that anything created under this Agreement which is an
Improvement to a party’s Pre-existing IP is and will be owned by that party owning the

relevant Pre-existing IP. At the intended owner’s cost, each party will do all things and
sign all instruments reasonably required to give effect to this clause 7.6.
7.7 All Data Outputs will be owned by and the exclusive property of the Company, and the
Service Provider will assign any and all of the Service Provider’s rights, title and interest
in and to the Data Outputs to the Company.
7.8 The Algorithm and any models used in relation to the Services will be owned by the
Service Provider.
7.9 The Company will grant the Service Provider a non-exclusive, royalty free license during
the term of this Agreement to use, copy, modify, enhance and sub-license any Pre-
existing IP of the Company to the extent that such use, copying, modification,
enhancement or sub-licensing is required to enable the Service Provider to perform the
Services.
7.10 The Service Provider warrants that, to the best of the Service Provider’s knowledge and
belief, the Created Materials will not contain any code or other materials that may
infringe the Intellectual Property Rights of any third party.
7.11 The obligations of confidentiality imposed upon the Service Provider will continue
indefinitely notwithstanding termination of this Agreement.
8. Non-solicitation / Non-dealing
8.1 Subject to clause 8.2, the Service Provider is free to undertake any work of any nature,
whether as an employee or as an independent Service Provider, provided that the
Service Provider continues to carry out the Services and comply with its obligations
under this Agreement. The Service Provider acknowledges the terms of clause 7 of this
Agreement apply when undertaking work for anyone other than the Company. Where
the Company has given an exclusive commitment to a client it will advise the Service
Provider of this. Where the Service Provider is carrying out work which would be in
conflict with that commitment it will advise the Company of this and the Company may
discontinue the Service Provider’s involvement with such client’s work.
8.2 The Service Provider shall not during the term of this Agreement and for the period of
time set out in Item 9 of the Schedule following the date of termination of the
Agreement either directly or indirectly for itself or on behalf of or in conjunction with
any other person:

(a) solicit or entice any employees or Service Providers of the Company to terminate
their employment or engagement with the Company, provided that this clause
will not prevent the Service Provider from employing an employee of the
Company that has responded without solicitation or enticement to a general
employment advertisement placed by the Service Provider; and
(b) solicit, entice, accept the custom of, provide services to (whether as an employee
or Service Provider) or deal with, any person, firm, organisation or company to
which the Service Provider has provided services under the terms of this
Agreement and where such person, firm, organisation or company remains a
client of the Company; and
(c) Whether as an employee or Service Provider or in any capacity, solicit, entice,
accept the custom of, provide services to or deal with any client of the Company
or provide services to or deal with any strategic partner of the Company or for
any company or organisation in which the Company has a financial interest,
whether the Service Provider has provided services to that client or company or
organisation during the term of the Agreement or not. “Any client” includes
anyone who has been a client of the Company in the 24 months immediately
preceding the date of termination of this Agreement and that the Service Provider
has engaged with through the performance of Services for the Company.
9. Termination
9.1 The Company may terminate this Agreement:
(a) at any time during its term upon giving the notice set out in Item 8 of the
Schedule, in writing to the Service Provider;
(b) immediately by written notice if the Service Provider is in breach of clause 7 or if
there is any other serious breach of this Agreement;
(c) immediately by written notice if the Service Provider is carrying out work which is
in conflict with any commitment made by the Company to a client as
contemplated in clause 8.1, the Company has discontinued the Service Provider’s
involvement with such client’s work and no other work is being carried out by the
Service Provider for the Company;
(d) if the Service Provider breaches any other term of this Agreement and fails to
remedy the breach within 10 Working Days of receipt of notice from the Company
requiring the breach to be remedied; or

(e) if the Service Provider becomes bankrupt, goes or is put into liquidation, has a
receiver or statutory manager appointed of any of its assets, becomes insolvent,
ceases to carry on its business or makes any composition or arrangement with its
creditors.
9.2 The Service Provider may terminate this Agreement:
(a) at any time during its term upon giving the notice set out in Item 8 in the Schedule
in writing to the Company; or
(b) if the Company breaches any other term of this Agreement and fails to remedy
the breach within 10 Working Days of receipt of notice from the Service Provider
requiring the breach to be remedied; or
(c) if the Company becomes bankrupt, goes or is put into liquidation, has a receiver
or statutory manager appointed of any of its assets, becomes insolvent, ceases to
carry on its business or makes any composition or arrangement with its creditors.
9.3 If this Agreement is terminated for any reason, the Service Provider will refund to the
Company, within 2 Working Days following termination, any amount paid by the
Company to the Service Provider pursuant to this Agreement in relation to Services that
have not been performed prior to termination.
9.4 Termination of this Agreement will be without prejudice to the rights and obligations
of the parties prior to termination and to any of the provisions of this Agreement which
are expressed to survive termination.
9.5 On termination of this agreement both parties will immediately return to the other all
documents, records, reports or other material, in paper or electronic form.
10. Disputes
10.1 If a dispute arises out of or relates to this Agreement (the Dispute) a party to the
Agreement may not commence any court or arbitration proceedings relating to the
Dispute unless it has complied with the following paragraphs of this clause except
where the party seeks urgent interlocutory relief.
10.2 A party claiming the Dispute has arisen under or in relation to this Agreement must
give written notice to the other party specifying the nature of the Dispute.

10.3 On receipt of that notice, the parties will use all reasonable endeavours to resolve the
Dispute by discussion, consultation, negotiation, or other informal means.
10.4 If the Dispute is not resolved within 15 Working Days of the notice being given pursuant
to clause 10.2 (or within such further period agreed in writing by the parties) either
party may seek resolution of the dispute by whatever means available at law.
11. Liability
11.1 The maximum aggregate liability of the Service Provider to the Company under or in
connection with this Agreement is limited to an amount equal to the total amount
paid by the Company to the Service Provider under this Agreement up to the date of
the first event giving rise to liability.
11.2 The Service Provider will not be liable to the Company under or in connection with
this Agreement for any loss of profit, revenue, savings, business or goodwill, or for any
consequential, indirect, incidental or special damage or loss of any kind.
11.3 The Company acknowledges that UniServices acts in respect of this Agreement solely
as agent for the Service Provider. To the extent permitted by law, UniServices will not
be liable to the Company for damage or loss of any kind in connection with this
Agreement, the Services or the Results, whether in contract, tort (including
negligence), breach of statutory duty or otherwise.
12. Publicity
12.1 Neither party may use the names, logos or trade marks of the other party, its Affiliates
or it or its Affiliates’ personnel in any publicity or marketing without the other party’s
prior written consent (and which, in the case of consent from the Service Provider,
may be withdrawn at any time).
13. General
13.1 Any notice to be given in terms of this Agreement must be made in writing, by email or
by post to the postal address, or by delivery to the registered office, of the other party
or to such other address as may be notified by either party to the other from time to
time. Any communication by email will be deemed to be received when transmitted to
the correct email address of the recipient. Any other communication in writing will be
deemed to be received when left at the registered office of the Company or if posted
to the postal address, on the third day following the date of posting. The initial, email,

postal, and physical registered office addresses of the Company and the Service
Provider are set out in Items 2 and 3 of the Schedule.
13.2 No waiver of any breach of this Agreement will be deemed to be a waiver of any other
or any subsequent breach. The failure of any party to enforce any provision of this
Agreement will not be interpreted as a waiver of the provision.
13.3 This Agreement, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes
all previous negotiations, commitments and/or writings, provided that, where a
confidentiality agreement has previously been signed by the parties, that confidentiality
agreement will continue in full force and effect, except to the extent of any
inconsistency with this Agreement.
13.4 No alteration of this Agreement will be binding unless it is in writing and executed by
both parties.
13.5 The Service Provider may not assign or transfer any of its rights or obligations under
this Agreement without the prior written consent of the Company.
13.6 If any provision of this Agreement is invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions
of this Agreement will not be affected and will continue in full force.
13.7 This Agreement may be executed by the parties in counterparts, each of which will be
deemed to be an original and all of which will constitute one and the same agreement.
13.8 This Agreement is subject to the laws of New Zealand and the parties submit to the
exclusive jurisdiction of the New Zealand courts. In addition to any other rights and
remedies available to the Company for breach of this Agreement, the Company will be
entitled to enforcement by court injunction or restraining order.
